title: Node 24, TypeScript 7, ESM, Dependencies, and Emitted Artifacts · TypeScript Build Room
summary: A disability-led public-interest technology team incrementally builds reproducible source-to-emitted crawler repository from original deterministic HTTP, robots, sitemap, HTML, browser, and report fixtures while retaining prior TypeScript, Node, web, Git, accessibility, security, testing, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of reproducible source-to-emitted crawler repository while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through predict…
objectives: Pin Node 24.18.0, TypeScript 7.0.2 and the TypeScript 6.0.2 compatibility API, ESM semantics, package manager, and supported platforms.
Pin Cheerio 1.2.0, robots-parser 3.0.1, p-limit 7.3.0, csv-stringify 6.8.1, Playwright 1.61.1, and axe-core 4.12.1 with provenance and license review.
Bind strict source, declarations, maps, generated fixtures, package exports, emitted JavaScript, and startup entry to one revision.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Runtime Matrix
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop TypeScript team handles case 1654 during an authorized municipal service audit. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reconcile Node, TypeScript, pinned crawler packages, emitted artifacts, clean gates, and recovery anchors; browser TypeScript remains deterministic and free of Node, network, filesystem, browser, and host effects; then change one HTML repair, Cheerio mode, selector, unknown value, record schema, link relation, or metadata case and retain rejected evidence. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Pin Node 24.18.0, TypeScript 7.0.2 and the TypeScript 6.0.2 compatibility API, ESM semantics, package manager, and supported platforms.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Runtime Matrix.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for pin node 24.18.0, typescript 7.0.2 and the typescript 6.0.2 compatibility api, esm semantics, package manager, and supported platforms. For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Runtime Matrix, keep Runtime Matrix connected to executable TypeScript authorization, URL, runtime admission, Fetch, Promise, browser, accessibility, report, package, and recovery decision evidence.
